outlook.headline: Memory supply stays tight as buildouts only slowly add capacity
outlook.summary: Mentions are still running above the trailing average (0.8x, +7%), and the reads point to steady tightness rather than a fresh shortage spike. TSMC, Micron, Applied Materials, Lam, Ultra Clean, Advanced Energy, Semtech, FormFactor and SanDisk all cite clean-room/tool bottlenecks or constrained DRAM output, while Entegris suggests meaningful wafer-start relief is more of a 2H26/2027 story; KLA and Amkor are neutral, and Intel is the lone bearish outlier on cost pressure.

drivers.label: Clean-room and tool constraints keep output tight
drivers.detail: Multiple companies say clean-room space, tool buildout, or utilization is still the pacing factor for memory production.

drivers.label: Capacity additions are underway but mostly not immediate
drivers.detail: New fabs, expansions, and added space should eventually lift supply, but the reads imply most of that relief is still ahead rather than in the next quarter.

drivers.label: Current usage remains strong
drivers.detail: Rising wafer volumes and higher utilization keep the supply chain constrained near term.

drivers.label: DRAM remains structurally constrained
drivers.detail: Management commentary points to supply-constrained DRAM and customer mix shifts to maximize opportunity.

risks: Clean-room/fab commissioning accelerates faster than expected, easing tightness sooner.
Utilization or wafer-volume growth stalls, reducing the supply constraint signal.
Customer capex delays or demand softening push out the buildout cycle.
